_comment: D179 Group 4 bounded flagship-named-units system. War Effort teaching/system only: no new playable battle, no tactical OOB rewrite, no Soldier's Story replacement, no super-unit bonus. Default bridge input is exact zero; optional unit stewardship is capped and costly.

US.name: Union colors and public memory
US.summary: The Union's flagship units include Black regiments, immigrant brigades, Western veterans in the Army of the Potomac, and small commands whose losses made the army's story personal.
US.teaching: This system makes famous formations visible without pretending they won battles alone. It ties identity, morale, pay, colors, casualties, and memory to the army the player fields.
sources: data/codex.json entries for 54th Massachusetts Infantry, Iron Brigade, Irish Brigade, United States Colored Troops, and Army of the Potomac.
data/soldier-replacements.json records for Chamberlain, Vincent, and Cushing, plus data/gettysburg.json OOB notes.

CS.name: Confederate unit memory without romance
CS.summary: Confederate unit identity is shown as real soldier memory and battlefield continuity while slavery, the Confederate cause, and Lost Cause mythmaking stay named and rejected.
CS.teaching: A famous Confederate brigade can be disciplined and costly without becoming moral cover. The system shows military identity and attrition while refusing heroic-exonerating memory.
sources: data/codex.json Stonewall Brigade and Army of Northern Virginia entries.
HISTORICAL-DATA.md First Bull Run OOB and Stonewall Brigade notes, plus data/chancellorsville.json Stonewall Brigade correction.

units.label: 54th Massachusetts Infantry
units.summary: One of the first Black regiments raised in the North, led by Col. Robert Gould Shaw and bloodied at Battery Wagner on July 18, 1863.
units.humanCost: Battery Wagner made the regiment's courage public, but unequal pay and capture policy made service a fight for citizenship as well as a fight under fire.
units.gameHook: Keeps the 54th visible as a flagship named unit without starting the later USCT playable battle arc early.
units.sourceNote: The local codex verifies the 54th Massachusetts with NPS and National Archives sources. No Battery Wagner playable battle is created here.
sources: data/codex.json 54th-massachusetts-infantry, citing National Park Service, The 54th Massachusetts and the Second Battle of Fort Wagner.
data/codex.json 54th-massachusetts-infantry, citing National Archives, 54th Massachusetts Casualty List.
data/codex.json robert-gould-shaw and united-states-colored-troops entries.

units.label: Iron Brigade
units.summary: Western Union regiments in black Hardee hats, remembered for South Mountain and for holding McPherson's Ridge on July 1 at Gettysburg.
units.humanCost: The brigade's reputation came at the price of depletion: Gettysburg shattered it as an effective formation.
units.gameHook: Connects Gettysburg and Antietam OOBs to a visible unit-memory ledger rather than a hidden badge only.
units.sourceNote: The local codex verifies the Iron Brigade with Nolan and Herdegen; Gettysburg and Antietam data already name the unit.
sources: data/codex.json iron-brigade, citing Alan T. Nolan, The Iron Brigade: A Military History.
data/codex.json iron-brigade, citing Lance J. Herdegen, The Iron Brigade in Civil War and Memory.
data/gettysburg.json and data/antietam.json current OOB notes.

units.label: Irish Brigade
units.summary: Irish-immigrant Union brigade associated with Thomas Francis Meagher, green colors, Antietam's Sunken Road, and Fredericksburg's Marye's Heights.
units.humanCost: Its celebrated identity was inseparable from heavy losses and immigrant sacrifice, not decorative ethnic flavor.
units.gameHook: Links manpower, immigrant service, Antietam, Fredericksburg, and the sourced McCarter Soldier's Story record.
units.sourceNote: The local codex verifies the Irish Brigade with ABT and Susannah Ural; McCarter supplies a verified human-scale tie.
sources: data/codex.json irish-brigade, citing American Battlefield Trust, Thomas Francis Meagher.
data/codex.json irish-brigade, citing Susannah J. Ural, The Harp and the Eagle.
data/soldier-replacements.json William McCarter record.

units.label: Stonewall Brigade
units.summary: Virginia brigade first commanded by Thomas J. Jackson, named at First Manassas and later nearly destroyed at Spotsylvania.
units.humanCost: The brigade's military reputation belongs beside its attrition and the Confederate cause it served, not inside Lost Cause romance.
units.gameHook: Makes the Bull Run and Chancellorsville Stonewall thread visible while preserving the disputed wording of Bee's quote.
units.sourceNote: The local codex verifies the unit with Encyclopedia Virginia and Robertson; HISTORICAL-DATA.md marks the exact quote as contested.
sources: data/codex.json stonewall-brigade, citing Encyclopedia Virginia, Stonewall Brigade.
data/codex.json stonewall-brigade, citing James I. Robertson Jr., The Stonewall Brigade.
HISTORICAL-DATA.md First Bull Run Stonewall Brigade and contested quote notes.

units.label: 20th Maine / Vincent's Brigade
units.summary: The 20th Maine held the extreme Union left on Little Round Top inside Strong Vincent's brigade on July 2, 1863.
units.humanCost: The bayonet charge matters, but the game keeps it inside a brigade decision and casualty chain rather than a one-man legend.
units.gameHook: Reuses the verified Chamberlain and Vincent Soldier's Story records as a unit-memory surface, with no new replacement row.
units.sourceNote: NPS OOB, NPS Little Round Top, and ABT sources already support the Chamberlain/Vincent records in data/soldier-replacements.json.
sources: data/soldier-replacements.json Joshua L. Chamberlain record, citing NPS Army of the Potomac OOB and NPS 20th Maine monument.
data/soldier-replacements.json Strong Vincent record, citing NPS Memory of Strong Vincent and American Battlefield Trust, Defense of Little Round Top.
data/gettysburg.json current OOB notes for the 20th Maine and Vincent's Brigade.

units.label: Battery A, 4th U.S. Artillery
units.summary: Alonzo Cushing's Battery A fought at the Angle on Cemetery Ridge during Pickett's Charge on July 3, 1863.
units.humanCost: The battery's stand is a sourced command-and-crew story, not an artillery damage bonus or a one-man victory explanation.
units.gameHook: Links the Gettysburg artillery slot, the universal gun model, and Cushing's verified Soldier's Story replacement.
units.sourceNote: NPS, Army.mil, and Congressional Medal of Honor Society sources already support the Cushing record in data/soldier-replacements.json.
sources: data/soldier-replacements.json Alonzo H. Cushing record, citing National Park Service, Alonzo Cushing at Gettysburg.
data/soldier-replacements.json Alonzo H. Cushing record, citing U.S. Army Medal of Honor narrative.
data/soldier-replacements.json Alonzo H. Cushing record, citing Congressional Medal of Honor Society recipient profile.

debates.title: Named units are not super-units
debates.summary: A famous formation can steady public memory and morale, but it cannot receive a hidden output gate or per-battle combat fudge.
sources: V1-CHECKLIST.md Group 4 contract for bounded non-battle systems and D74 universal combat guard.
DECISIONS.md D178 bridge contract for exact-zero default, capped explicit War Effort inputs.

debates.title: Human cost through formations
debates.summary: Losses become legible through regiments, brigades, batteries, colors, and letters rather than only aggregate casualty totals.
sources: AUTONOMOUS-RUN.md D35.6 human-cost-with-gravity directive.
V1-CHECKLIST.md D38.3 flagship named units tie human cost and perspectives.

debates.title: 54th visible now, USCT battles later
debates.summary: The 54th Massachusetts can be visible as a flagship unit now, but this slice does not start any USCT playable battle.
sources: V1-CHECKLIST.md Group 7 keeps USCT playable battles last unless Aaron explicitly reorders.
data/codex.json 54th-massachusetts-infantry and united-states-colored-troops entries.
